mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k per month indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in residential construction (HOUST) below 1.5 million units signals weak housing demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as economic growth prospects deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST falls below 1.5 million units.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 followed by a rebound indicates supply chain disruptions and subsequent recovery, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances following INDPRO declines exceeding 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signal disinflationary pressures and monetary easing, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved real rate expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real estate finance (RSAFS) above $600 billion combined with rising GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong private sector demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when both RSAFS exceeds 600B and GDPC1 grows >1% q/q.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5% combined with declining payrolls (PAYEMS) signals economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive when both UNRATE exceeds 5% and PAYEMS falls >50k.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 followed by a recovery in housing starts (HOUST) indicates supply-side stabilization, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when INDPRO falls below 100 and HOUST increases >5%.
